phrasal_verbs.meaning: stop demanding sth, stop saying that you will do sth
give to a person in authority
introduce a new law or system
give little or no punishment; make a bomb, etc explode
follow sb/sth quickly in order to catch them
explode; be fired (for a gun, usually accidentally)
trick sb into believing sth that is not true

permission: have/ask for/get permission (from sb) to do
solution: have/find/think of/work out/come up with/figure out a solution (to sth)
threaten: to do, threaten sb with sth

B.title: Original course practice
B.instructions: Complete each original item using the target language from this lesson.

across.clue: A person who causes disturbance and trouble in public, especially at sports events. (8)
The formal decision reached by a judge or jury at the end of a trial. (7)
Someone who takes things belonging to others without permission. (5)
Information or objects used in a court of law to help prove whether something is true. (8)
A person who the police believe may be responsible for a crime. (7)
The jury found her not ... of the charges against her. (6)
To officially accuse someone of a crime and take legal action against them in court. (9)

down.clue: A professional who is qualified to advise on legal matters and represent people in court. (6)
To take someone into police custody when you believe they have committed a crime. (6)
A person who deliberately damages or destroys public or private property. (6)
If you are not guilty of a crime, you are described as this. (8)
To formally accuse someone of committing a crime so they must appear in court. (6)
To treat a person or group cruelly and unfairly, often because of their beliefs or identity. (9)
The person who is on trial in a court of law, also known as the defendant. (7)
